V.rationale: Best-in-class payments compounder with elite margins/ROE trading at a ~7% pullback from recent highs, offering a modestly attractive risk/reward despite the absence of explicit valuation multiples to confirm cheapness.
key_factors: Exceptional quality: operating_margin=0.645, net_margin=0.519, ROE=0.424 — best-in-class among large-cap financials
Constructive pullback: stock declined ~7% from $248.11 (2023-09-01) to $230.01 (2023-09-29), improving entry after +27.7% 1-year run
Sequential revenue growth: $7.94B → $7.99B → $8.12B across Q1-Q3 FY23, modest but consistent top-line expansion
Bullish news sentiment: multiple buy-themed articles (Is Visa a Buy?, Magnificent Stocks I'm Never Selling, Perfect Growth Stocks) on 2023-09-29/30

CSCO.rationale: Fair valuation and solid fundamentals offset by Splunk acquisition integration risk; recent pullback creates a modestly better entry but no clear edge.
key_factors: P/E 17.5x sits in the middle of 1y range (13.9-20.7) — neither cheap nor expensive
Strong fundamentals: net margin 22.1%, ROE 28.4%, D/E 0.189, gross margin 62.7%
Quarterly revenue trajectory positive: $13.59B → $14.57B → $15.20B (Q2→Q4 FY23)
Recent ~6% pullback from $57.84 (9/1) to $53.76 (9/29) coincides with Splunk deal announcement (9/21)
